title: Scilex Holding Company Announces Dream Bowl I Meme Coin Tokens to List on Biconomy Exchange as Early as June 23, 2026
description: PALO ALTO, Calif., June 16, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Scilex Holding Company (“Scilex” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: SCLX), an innovative revenue-generating company focused on acquiring, developing, and commercializing non-opioid pain management products for the treatment of acute and chronic pain and neurodegenerative and cardiometabolic disease, today announced Dream Bowl I Meme Coin tokens (“Dream Bowl Tokens”) to list on Biconomy Exchange as early as June 23, 2026.

title: Bombardier Delivers First Global 8000 in Africa to Nigeria-based BUA Group
description: MONTREAL, June 16, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bombardier today announced the delivery of the first Global 8000 aircraft in Africa to BUA Group, a Nigerian-based multinational company with interests across cement, food, oil and gas, energy, infrastructure, ports and terminals, and real estate. The aircraft will support BUA Group’s long-range travel requirements, enabling seamless non-stop connections from Lagos to key business destinations around the world, including Los Angeles, Pert

title: Bombardier livre le premier avion Global 8000 en Afrique à BUA Group, au Nigéria
description: MONTRÉAL, 16 juin 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bombardier a annoncé aujourd’hui la livraison du premier avion Global 8000 en Afrique à BUA Group, une multinationale basée au Nigéria qui détient des intérêts dans les secteurs du ciment, de l’agroalimentaire, du pétrole et du gaz, de l’énergie, des infrastructures, des ports et terminaux, ainsi que de l’immobilier. title: Ukraine’s demand for tiny drone laser-targeting systems spurs new product launches
description: Companies rush to miniaturize the technology for marking targets on the battlefield, making it suitable to ride on small unmanned aerial vehicles.

title: India touts new ballistic missile defense prowess after tests
description: India aims to joint a small club of countries capable of bringing down incoming missiles with technology made domestically.

title: General Atomics awarded US Army contract for extended-range artillery round
description: During a test last year, the company's projectile hit targets more than 74 miles away after being fired from an M777 howitzer.

title: Russian strategic bomber crashes in Siberia during training exercise
description: The Soviet-era supersonic bomber has most recently been used by Russia for combat missions in Syria and Ukraine.

title: Iran, US agree to halt war and reopen Hormuz
description: The memorandum of understanding is scheduled to be officially signed on Friday in Switzerland.

title: Australia forges ahead with 155mm ammo production
description: The Maryborough factory will commence M795 production by late 2028 at a rate of 15,000 rounds annually.

title: France picks MBDA-Safran combo to supply multiple rocket launcher
description: Paris snubbed competing offers from Thales and ArianeGroup, as well as from U.S. defense giant Lockheed Martin and South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace.

title: French army shows Leclerc tank with once-mocked anti-drone cage
description: Once derided as primitive, roof-mounted armor variants have been adopted by both Russian and Ukrainian forces as drone threats proliferate.

title: France prepares Ukraine-inspired ‘kill web’ for battlefield awareness
description: The kill web will be part of the broader Arcadia AI-powered battlefield command and control structure, a top French commander said.

title: Interview: French Army chief Schill talks technology, surprise and ‘archaic’ combat
description: "We should avoid excessive analogies. France is not Ukraine," said Gen. Pierre Schill in an interview ahead of the Eurosatory defense expo near Paris.

title: US strike kills leader of Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua gang, White House says
description: Venezuela’s information ministry said that during the operation there were clashes with members of criminal groups, in which the leader was neutralized.

title: Only 1 in 4 F-35s is fully mission capable, GAO finds
description: The F-35’s readiness rates continued to decline through fiscal 2025, with the fleet’s full mission capable rate falling to 25%, according to a GAO report.

title: US Army commissions second cohort of tech executives into innovation unit
description: This week, the Army added three more technology executives to Detachment 201, a unit intended to bridge the gap between the commercial sector and military.

title: Ukraine’s defense AI chief predicts ‘new paradigm’ of warfare
description: “The system that possesses more data and better understands that data, proposes solutions — that system will gain the advantage over the other."

title: US plans major cut to jets, warships for NATO operations in Europe, NYT reports
description: The United States reportedly plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe.

title: Humvee maker AM General to pitch unmanned ground vehicle at Eurosatory trade show
description: The Ukrainian military aims to contract 25,000 unmanned ground platforms in the first half of this year, more than doubling its 2025 total.

title: Paris defense show may be last chance to buy before war, CEO says
description: Surging defense spending means Eurosatory is bigger than ever this year, with the number of exhibitors rising 30% to more than 2,600, per the organizer.

title: Trump vows to seize Iran’s Kharg Island
description: Kharg Island, the linchpin of Iran’s oil industry, has once more come under focus amid a fraying ceasefire.

title: UK defense secretary resigns in protest of government spending plan
description: In his resignation letter to Starmer, Healey said he had now seen the defense funding plan, and it “falls well short of what is required."

title: German, Spanish FCAS companies rally to preserve breakthrough fighter tech
description: With existing FCAS contracts ending this year, the companies want the government to keep spending money on a sixth-generation warplane.

title: ‘They got very lucky,’ Trump says of downed Apache helicopter’s crew
description: After a U.S. Army Apache helicopter was downed by an Iranian drone, President Donald Trump said the rescued aviators “got very lucky.”

title: US Navy stands up naval support activity in Western Australia
description: As part of the AUKUS security pact, NSA Stirling will provide support for personnel assigned to Submarine Rotational Force-West.

title: US Army astronaut tapped for NASA’s Artemis III mission
description: The U.S. Army is launching into space again by way of Col. Frank Rubio, who will be part of the 2027 Artemis III mission, NASA announced Tuesday.

title: Qatari-donated Air Force One now sports red, white and blue paint job
description: The interim Air Force One VC-25B Bridge aircraft is undergoing its final modifications ahead of a summer rollout.

description: This podcast explores how leading Alzheimer’s experts are working to bridge the gap between clinical trial design and frontline care.

title: Medicare drug price rule may target under-the-skin Keytruda and Opdivo
description: For 2029, a federal rule proposes closing a "loophole" that protects medicines transitioning from intravenous administration from price protection.

title: Lilly scores early positive data for new JAK drug
description: Acquired through its purchase of Ajax, the drug showed encouraging effects in patients with hard-to-treat myelofibrosis. To analysts, such results could lead to competition for Incyte’s Jakafi.

title: J&J multiple myeloma drug gains edge in earlier stage disease
description: The latest results studying Talvey in combination with Darzalex Faspro support J&J’s efforts to expand the immunotherapy's use.

title: Celiac disease is entering a new era of therapeutic innovation
description: For the ~1 in 100 people living with celiac, the burden of disease extends far beyond their plate.

title: Planning a successful pivot from lab to clinic
description: For sterile injectables, key early decisions can have a significant impact on the products’ development.

title: Rapport ‘fully prepared’ to launch seizure drug solo, CEO says
description: In a wide-ranging interview, Abe Ceesay detailed how Rapport built an investor syndicate and why the company is confident it can join the ranks of successful, commercial-stage brain drugmakers.

title: Summit pulls $500M share sale a day after announcing it
description: The developer of a leading PD-1/VEGF cancer drug cited “market conditions” for the cancellation. Its shares have struggled to break out following data milestones.

title: Sensorion, citing Regeneron competition, shifts focus to a different hearing loss therapy
description: A strategic review determined that the development environment had "notably changed," leading Sensorion to end work on its gene therapy for OTOF-related hearing loss.

title: Novartis RNA drug acquired in $12B Avidity deal notches a trial win
description: The data showed that del-brax, an “antisense oligonucleotide conjugate” being tested against a muscle-wasting disease, met its primary endpoint in a Phase 1/2 study.
